description: re-baseline champion: mw_commit_throughput median-of-7=22182/s (MAXIMIZE; samples 20671-22556, +-4% tight); mw_commit_p99=192153ns; conflict_rate=0.73 (stable workload sensor); gate e2e_p50=559174ns e2e_p99=1315109ns PASS; torture 5/5 + Gate A green. Establishes multiwriter-commit baseline

merge slow-path dedup: thread merge_keys_from's already-fetched prior into new upsert_arc_with_prior, removing the redundant per-key get_arc descent: mw_commit_throughput median-of-7=22190/s vs champ 22182 (WASH +0.04%; 20717-22769 overlaps champ 20671-22556); conflict_rate 0.74 (stable, comparison valid); 535 tests+OCC/index+torture 5/5+smoke green. DISCARD: eliminated get_arc is a cheap read descent (no alloc); merge cost is the CoW write (insert_arc+index on_update), unchanged. Net-adds helper

remove per-commit snapshot table-map rebuild alloc (clone latest tables BTreeMap + apply merged via Vec instead of fresh-map collect; fold merged_tables into Vec): mw_commit_throughput median-of-7=20996/s vs champ 22182 (WASH/noise, -5%; 20218-22455 overlaps champ 20671-22556, batch-median variance ~5-6%); conflict_rate 0.74 (stable); 535 tests+OCC/MVCC+torture 5/5+smoke green. DISCARD: single-table workload -> the rebuilt map is 1 entry, so the removed alloc is sub-noise (cf smr Arc-map analysis). No primary gain

lock-free rolled-back WriteTx::drop (atomic active_writer_count moved to Store; MultiWriter base-version cleanup deferred to an uncontended queue drained lazily under inner by next begin/commit): mw_commit_throughput median-of-7=21879/s vs champ 22182 (WASH -1.4%; 20210-22373 overlaps champ 20671-22556); conflict 0.74 stable; full suite+single-writer-enforcement+MultiWriter+SSI+GC+mock_wal+torture 5/5+smoke green. DISCARD: removing a write-lock ACQUISITION on 74% of attempts didn't move throughput -> bottleneck is inner.write() HOLD TIME during begin/commit-install (serialized snapshot install), not acquisition count

re-baseline champion on jun18 sandbox host: mw_commit_throughput=22481/s (single gated run; recorded champ median-of-7=22182 reproduces within noise); mw_commit_p99=203321ns; mw_scaling_8x=21205/s mw_scaling_efficiency=0.438 (negative scaling, lock-hold-time bound); gate e2e_p50=6422806ns e2e_p99=7083222ns PASS (host ~11x slower than prior champ host, re-baselined); torture 5/5 + Gate A 535 green. Establishes jun18 host baseline

move snapshot table-map clone OFF the held inner.write() lock in commit_multi_writer Phase 3 (capture phase1_base_version; prebuild map off-lock from latest_tables_ref + merged/deleted; fast-path install when latest unchanged, slow-path re-fork preserved): mw_commit_throughput median-of-5=22342/s vs champ 22481 (WASH -0.6%; samples 20784-22741 overlap champ); mw_scaling_8x median ~20581 (-2.9%, noise); gate e2e_p50=6544458 +1.9% PASS; torture 5/5 + OCC/MVCC/SSI + clippy green. DISCARD: the frozen microbench is SINGLE-TABLE (disjoint KEYS on one 'state' table), so the snapshot table-map is 1 entry and moving its clone off-lock is sub-noise (same as prior single-table map-rebuild discard). Optimization is real for multi-table workloads the microbench cannot exercise; adds a fast/slow branch for no gated gain

gc_inner bounded-candidate prune (strong_count only the oldest len-retain_count snapshots instead of full-window retain scan; exact retention preserved, all GC tests green): mw_commit_throughput median-of-5=22391/s vs champ 22481 (WASH -0.4%; first run-iter sample 23507 was a high outlier; range 21840-23507); p99 median ~191K (vs 203K, slightly better noisy); mw_disjoint one-off 12243 in run-iter was a fluke (standalone samples 20527-23652 normal); mw_scaling_8x median 20959 (-1.2%); gate e2e_p50=6577888 +2.4% PASS; torture 5/5 + retention + clippy green. DISCARD: profiling over-attributed GC to wall-clock — lock WAIT is only 0.2%, writers don't block on inner.write(), so cutting in-lock CPU (GC strong_count) doesn't raise aggregate throughput. Real bottleneck is OFF-LOCK per-commit work: index maint 42.2% + CoW btree 15.6%. Correctness-neutral micro-opt, no primary gain. Next: attack index maintenance
